info.title: Kentico Xperience Database Manager
info.description: Provides tooling to install, initialize and maintain databases for Xperience by Kentico projects.

commands.name: install
commands.description: Install the database for Xperience by Kentico.

options.name: --admin-password
options.description: Sets the password for the default 'administrator' account.

options.name: --server-name
options.description: Name of the SQL server to connect to.

options.name: --connection-timeout
options.description: Database connection timeout in seconds.

options.name: --database-name
options.description: Name of the database to create.

options.name: --hash-string-salt
options.description: Custom salt used for the hash function to create the macro signatures.

options.name: --instance-friendly-name
options.description: Represents the instance in Client Portal. If left empty, a default numeric identifier is used instead. Friendly names may only contain letters, numbers, spaces, and the special characters: .,?!|'\/-_

options.name: --license-file
options.description: The absolute or relative path of the license file containing the license key for the site.

options.name: --password
options.description: Password used for SQL server authentication.

options.name: --recreate-existing-database
options.description: Indicates if the database is recreated if it already exists. Otherwise use '--use-existing-database' option or the installation is terminated.

options.name: --sample-email-service-domain
options.description: The Email service domain set for the sample email channel. Use this parameter if you want to override the default 'dancinggoat.ai' service domain for the sample email channel. The parameter is ignored if no sample website exists in the database.

options.name: --sample-website-domain
options.description: The domain to be configured for the sample website. 'Localhost' will serve as the default value if not provided. The option is ignored if no sample website exists in the database.

options.name: --use-existing-database
options.description: Indicates if the database should be used if it already exists. Otherwise use '--recreate-existing-database' option or the installation is terminated.

options.name: --username
options.description: Username used for SQL server authentication. If not provided, windows authentication is used to connect to the SQL server.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Indicates if verbose logging is enabled.

options.name: --server-name
options.description:
Required. Name of the SQL server to connect
to.

options.name: --database-name
options.description:
(Default: xperience) Name of the database to
create.

options.name: --username
options.description:
Username used for SQL server authentication.
If not provided, windows authentication is
used to connect to the SQL server.

options.name: --password
options.description: Password used for SQL server authentication.

options.name: --admin-password
options.description:
Required. Sets the password for the default
'administrator' account.

options.name: --hash-string-salt
options.description:
Custom salt used for the hash function to
create the macro signatures.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Indicates if verbose logging is enabled.

options.name: --recreate-existing-database
options.description:
Indicates if the database is recreated if it
already exists. Otherwise use
'--use-existing-database' option or the
installation is terminated.

options.name: --use-existing-database
options.description:
Indicates if the database should be used if
it already exists. Otherwise use
'--recreate-existing-database' option or the

options.name: --license-file
options.description:
The absolute or relative path of the license
file containing the license key for the site.

options.name: --connection-timeout
options.description:
(Default: 60) Database connection timeout in
seconds.

options.name: --sample-website-domain
options.description:
(Default: localhost) The domain to be
configured for the sample website.
'Localhost' will serve as the default value
if not provided. The option is ignored if no
sample website exists in the database.

options.name: --sample-email-service-domain
options.description:
The Email service domain set for the sample
email channel. Use this parameter if you want
to override the default 'dancinggoat.ai'
service domain for the sample email channel.
The parameter is ignored if no sample website
exists in the database.

options.name: --instance-friendly-name
options.description:
Represents the instance in Client Portal. If
left empty, a default numeric identifier is
used instead. Friendly names may only contain
letters, numbers, spaces, and the special
characters: .,?!|'\/-_

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.
